name: Chestnut Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Whip the egg whites until they become frothy.
2. Gradually add the brown rice syrup to the whipped egg whites and mix well. The mixture will lose some lift but will still work for baking.
3. In a separate bowl, combine the flour and finely ground roasted chestnuts until evenly mixed.
4. Gradually fold the flour mixture into the egg white mixture until fully incorporated.
5. Drop spoonfuls of the dough onto parchment paper-lined cookie sheets or regular cookie sheets lightly coated with oil.
6. Bake at 400°F for about 10 minutes, or until golden brown and crisp around the edges.
7. Allow cookies to cool on the baking sheet for a few minutes before transferring them to wire racks to cool completely.
This recipe yields approximately 18-20 large cookies. The texture is chewy, with an old-fashioned flavor that varies slightly depending on the sweetness of the chestnuts used. These cookies become more enjoyable as you eat more of them.
